title: Neoporteria totoralensis
intro: Neoporteria totoralensis, also known as Parastrephia lepidophora, is a plant species native to the Andean region, belonging to the Asteraceae family and commonly referred to as a type of thistle. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis. The bioactive constituents of Neoporteria totoralensis include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and teas, as well as being incorporated into ointments and creams, which are used topically to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
